codeReview.summary: The fix adds a new test file, but it still does not implement runtime log-capture assertions against actual emitted startup/sync logs. Most assertions validate stored metadata and direct redactor helper output, so regressions in real logging callsites can still pass undetected.

issues.description: The core capture helper (`captureStoreOutputs`) only inspects serialized metadata and database rows; it does not attach a log sink or capture emitted logs from startup/sync paths. This does not satisfy VAL-CROSS-006 runtime log-capture requirements.

The redaction coverage remains helper-level: the test manually calls `CLILaunchAdapter.filterAllowlistedEnvironment` and `CLILaunchRedactor.redactEnvironment` instead of executing logging-producing startup/sync flows and asserting on emitted log lines.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `followedProcedure=true`, but the handoff still lacks context-read evidence required by the skill's handoff-discipline section.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md requires concrete read-evidence commands when `followedProcedure=true`; this handoff's `verification.commandsRun` only lists two test commands plus `git rev-parse HEAD`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill step 6 requires collecting log evidence for integration hardening, but the implementation and handoff evidence stayed at helper/store assertions and did not provide emitted-log traces.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md step 6 calls for UI/state/launch/log evidence; new tests center on DB/store outputs and direct redactor calls (SwitcherRuntimeLogCaptureTests.swift lines 98-127 and 401-442).

summary: Fix review failed. Although the new suite increases secret-safety assertions, it still does not capture and assert real runtime startup/sync log emission, so the prior VAL-CROSS-006 failure is not adequately resolved.
